coredata.dc:title: Naked-eye-based highly selective sensing of Fe3+ and further for PPi with nano copolymer film
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2015 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Zhanxian
Li, Haixia
Shi, Caixia
Zhang, Wenying
Zhou, Wan
Wei, Liuhe
Yu, Mingming
coredata.dc:description: Novel rhodamine and quinoline-functionalized nano film was prepared by copolymerization and electrospinning. Propenyl monomer of rhodamine and quinoline was synthesized and then copolymerized with methyl methacrylate via solution polymerization. This prepared copolymer was electrospun into nano film, as a solid-state sensor, which exhibits highly selective recognition of Fe3+ ions over various environmentally and biologically relevant metal ions and anions with a distinct color change from colorless to pink in very fast response time (<1min). The sensor can detect Fe3+ quantitatively in concentration range of 100–2000μM, and the calculated low detection limit (LOD) value could be 1.19μM for Fe3+. As the resultant product, the Fe3+-contaminated nano film showed recovered color change by extra addition of pyrophosphate P2O7 4− (PPi) solution. It was found that the absorption intensity at 564nm of the Fe3+-contaminated nano film linearly decreases with PPi concentration from 50 to 3000μM with LOD value of 1.75μM. In addition, both the nano films before and after Fe3+-contaminating are reusable for the detection of Fe3+ and PPi, respectively.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Nano film
Copolymerization
Electrospinning
Sensor
Ferric ion
Pyrophosphate

co
-RQ) nano film

co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+

co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+

co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+

Counterion effect on the Fe3+-selective properties of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film

Colorimetric sensing properties of Fe3+-containing poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward PPi

co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+ and further PPi

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: The authors thank National Natural Science Foundation of China ( U1504203 and J1210060 ), Key Laboratory of Photochemical Conversion and Optoelectronic Materials and Zhengzhou University for financial support. Appendix A

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM image of poly(MMA-co-RQ) nano particles by electrospinning 5wt% poly(MMA-co-RQ) DMF solution (left, 10μm; right, 1μm). Inset show further magnified image of the electrospun nanofilm.
Absorption (up) spectra change of the film and photographs of the film (down) in daylight in the presence of Fe3+ (0–20μM) at different concentrations.
Absorption spectra response of the film at 563nm as a linear dependence within the concentration of Fe3+. A represents the absorbance at 563nm.

Absorption (up) spectra change of the film and photographs of the film (down) in daylight in the presence of different metal ions (5×10−3 M). The metal ions are Fe3+, Al3+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, K+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Cr3+.

Absorption spectra response of the film at 563nm in the presence of different metal ions (5×10−3 M) (red bars) and absorption spectra response of the film containing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) after addition of other metal ions (5×10−3 M). A and A 0 represent the absorbance at 563nm. (For interpretation of the references to color in figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of the article.)

Absorption spectra response of the film at 563nm in the presence of different anions (5×10−3 M) (red bars) and absorption spectra response of the film containing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) after addition of different anions (concentration) (up), and the anions from 1 to 21 are P2O7 4−, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −. A and A 0 represent the absorbance at 563nm. Photographs of the film (down) in daylight in the presence of Fe3+ and different anions (0–20μM). The species within the film from left to right are none, Fe3+, Fe3+ and PPi, Fe3+ and F−, Fe3+ and Cl−, Fe3+ and Br−, Fe3+ and I−, Fe3+ and H2PO4 −, Fe3+ and CH3COO−, Fe3+ and HSO4 −, Fe3+ and NO3 −, Fe3+ and SCN−, Fe3+ and SO4 2−, Fe3+ and HPO4 2−, Fe3+ and S2−, Fe3+ and NO2 −, Fe3+ and SO3 2−, Fe3+ and HSO3 −, Fe3+ and ClO4 −, Fe3+ and N3 −, Fe3+ and SiO3 2−, Fe3+ and S2O3 2−, Fe3+ and ClO3 −. (For interpretation of the references to color in figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of the article.)

Absorption (up) spectra change of the film containing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) and photographs of the film containing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) (down) in daylight in the presence of PPi (the concentrations from left to right are 0, 1×10−5 M, 5×10−5 M, 9×10−5 M, 1×10−4 M, 3×10−4 M, 5×10−4 M, 7×10−4 M, 9×10−4 M, 1×10−3 M, 2×10−3 M, 3×10−3 M, 4×10−3 M, 5×10−3 M, 6×10−3 M, 7×10−3 M). Inset: absorption spectra response of the film containing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) at 563nm as a linear dependence within the concentration of PPi.

Absorption spectra response of the Fe3+-containing film at 563nm in the presence of different anions (concentration) (red bars) and absorption spectra response of the film containing Fe3+ (concentration) and PPi (concentration) after addition of other different anions (concentration) (up). The anions from 1 to 21 are PPi, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −. Photographs of the film (down) in daylight in the presence of Fe3+, PPi and other different anions (0–20μM). The species within the film from left to right are none; Fe3+; Fe3+ and PPi; Fe3+, PPi and F−; Fe3+, PPi and Cl−; Fe3+, PPi and Br−; Fe3+, PPi and I−; Fe3+, PPi and H2PO4 −; Fe3+, PPi and CH3COO−; Fe3+, PPi and HSO4 −; Fe3+, PPi and NO3 −; Fe3+, PPi and SCN−; Fe3+, PPi and SO4 2−; Fe3+, PPi and HPO4 2−; Fe3+, PPi and S2−; Fe3+, PPi and NO2 −; Fe3+, PPi and SO3 2−; Fe3+, PPi and HSO3 −; Fe3+, PPi and ClO4 −; Fe3+, PPi and N3 −; Fe3+, PPi and SiO3 2−; Fe3+, PPi and S2O3 2−; Fe3+, PPi and ClO3 −. (For interpretation of the references to color in figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of the article.)

Synthesis route of RQ and poly(MMA-co-RQ). (I) NH2NH2·H2O, CH3OH, reflux, 76.9%; (II) acryloyl chloride, triethylamine, toluene, 0–20°C, 47.3%; (III) SeO2, 1,4-dioxane, reflux, 71.6%; (IV) C2H5OH, reflux, 84.7%; (V) MMA, AIBN, DMF, 70°C.
Possible sensing mechanism of the film toward Fe3+ and further for PPi.

head.ce:title: Naked-eye-based highly selective sensing of Fe3+ and further for PPi with nano copolymer film

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University, Zhengzhou 450001, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Novel rhodamine-functionalized electrospun film was synthesized and utilized as colorimetric chemosensor for Fe3+ and further for PPi for the first time. This prepared copolymer was electrospun into nano film, as a solid-state sensor, which exhibits highly selective recognition of Fe3+ ions with a distinct color change from colorless to pink in very fast response time (<1min). As the resultant product, the Fe3+-contaminated nano film showed recovered color change by extra addition of PPi solution.

Novel rhodamine and quinoline-functionalized nano film was prepared by copolymerization and electrospinning. Propenyl monomer of rhodamine and quinoline was synthesized and then copolymerized with methyl methacrylate via solution polymerization. This prepared copolymer was electrospun into nano film, as a solid-state sensor, which exhibits highly selective recognition of Fe3+ ions over various environmentally and biologically relevant metal ions and anions with a distinct color change from colorless to pink in very fast response time (<1min). The sensor can detect Fe3+ quantitatively in concentration range of 100–2000μM, and the calculated low detection limit (LOD) value could be 1.19μM for Fe3+. As the resultant product, the Fe3+-contaminated nano film showed recovered color change by extra addition of pyrophosphate P2O7 4− (PPi) solution. It was found that the absorption intensity at 564nm of the Fe3+-contaminated nano film linearly decreases with PPi concentration from 50 to 3000μM with LOD value of 1.75μM. In addition, both the nano films before and after Fe3+-contaminating are reusable for the detection of Fe3+ and PPi, respectively.

ce:section.ce:para: In recent years, there has been significant interest in the design and development of artificial optical chemosensors toward environmentally and biologically important analysts such as metal ions and anions. Among biologically important metal ions, Fe3+ is an essential element in the human body and provides the oxygen-carrying capacity of heme and acts as a cofactor in many enzymatic reactions. The trivalent form of iron plays an important role in many biological processes such as oxygen metabolism to DNA. Fe3+ is indispensable for most organisms, and both its deficiency and excess result in various pathological disorders [1,2]. On the other hand, as the product of ATP hydrolysis under cellular conditions, pyrophosphate P2O7 4− (PPi) is involved in DNA replication catalyzed by DNA polymerase and its detection has become more and more important in cancer research in the recent years [3–5]. Therefore, it is significant to develop optical chemosensors toward Fe3+ and PPi.

Fluorescent sensors have attracted great attention of scientists due to their simplicity, high sensitivity and instantaneous response [6], and as a result, many fluorescent chemosensors toward Fe3+ [7–16] and PPi [17–26] have been reported. Among them, nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots (N-GQDs), through the hydrothermal treatment of GQDs with hydrazine, show fluorescence emission quenching behavior at 442 and 435nm [13]. Other S-doped carbon dots [27], N-doped carbon dots [28,29] and N-doped graphene quantum dots [14] reported all show quenching behavior toward Fe3+ ions. In general, fluorescent sensors depend on emission intensity or position power and detector sensitivity, to contrast, color changes based on absorption properties of the ground state are more suitable for direct observation with the naked eye [30], and considerable efforts have been devoted to designing colorimetric chemosensors toward Fe3+ [31–34] and PPi [35–37] in solution during the past few decades. However, the challenge to prepare practical and cost-effective sensors still remains. Owing to large surface area and high porosity, electrospinning is an effective way for preparing various composite nanofibers. Compared with dye-doping or other physical methods, it is better to connect the fluorescent group to the polymer chain by covalent bond to avoid aggregation and self-quenching of molecular fluorophores, fluorophore leakage, and inner-layer analyte diffusion [15]. The response time would be very fast for the reason that analytes need not diffuse to the inner-layer. Conventionally, the classical design of a colorimetric sensor includes two fundamental moieties, a receptor in charge of the recognition and a chromophore responsible to signal the recognition event. Water-insolubility results in organic solvent dependence and limits the practicality of the most reported sensors [38]. Besides, the conventional chemical sensors are used in solution, which may interfere with the cell or tissue and release chemicals. That is to say, such a sensor is invasive. Compared with the conventional chemical sensors, a solid-state sensor is non-invasive and it transforms the real-time chemical concentration into electronic signals without interference with the biological processes under study [39]. To date, most of the studies on the Fe3+ and PPi-sensors are stressed on the design of selective fluorescent and colorimetric sensors dissolved in aqueous or polar organic solvents. In contrast, the investigation of sensors on the solid matrices has been rarely studied [40–43].

As an effective and simple method for preparing various composite nanofibers, the electrospinning technique has been developed for the synthesis of nanofibers since 1934 [44–46]. The process of electrospinning uses a strong electrostatic force by a high static voltage applied to a polymer solution placed into a container that has a millimeter diameter nozzle. Under applied electrical force, the polymer solution is ejected from the nozzle. After the solvents are evaporated, the nanofibers or nanoparticles are collected on a grounded collector [47]. Because nano films prepared by electrospinning have large surface area-to-volume ratio, unique nanoscale architecture, and simple and cost-effective preparation method, they have been proved to be excellent host materials for developing functional materials [48]. In addition, nano films can have more surface area than that found in conventional thin film, which is expected to have the potential to provide unusually high sensitivity and fast response time in sensing application [49]. As a result, electrospun film is becoming more and more popular as matrices for optical sensors [50–52]. Unfortunately, the reported solid state sensor by electrospinning toward Fe3+ and PPi are very rare [15,41,42] and the LOD is not low enough, so it is important to develop electrospun solid state sensors for Fe3+ and PPi.

Rhodamine derivatives are extensively employed in fluorescent and colorimetric chemosensors in solution because of their high absorption coefficient, high fluorescence quantum yield and long-wavelength absorption and emission. With the development of the electrospun solid-state sensors, some rhodamine-based electrospun films have been prepared to sense Hg2+ [53,54] and Cu2+ [55] and no examples about the electrospun film sensors for Fe3+ and PPi have been reported.

In this work, novel rhodamine-functionalized electrospun film was synthesized and utilized as colorimetric chemosensor for Fe3+ and further for PPi for the first time. The nano film showed high sensitivity and selectivity toward Fe3+ and further toward PPi over other metal ions and anions. The sensing material poly(MMA-co-RQ) was synthesized by copolymerizing rhodamine functionalized (RQ) with methyl methacrylate (MMA) (Scheme 1 ).

Rhodamine (95%) and acryloyl chloride (analytical grades) were purchased from Sigma–Aldrich Inc., USA. SeO2 (99%) was obtained from the Aladdin Reagent Co., Ltd., China. 1,4-Dioxane (analytical grade) was purchased from Tianjin Kermel Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd., China, hydrazine hydrate (analytical grade) from Tianjin Jiangtian Chemical Technology Co., Ltd., China, methanol and ethanol (gas chromatography grade) from Tianjin Siyou Fine Chemicals Co., Ltd., China. Other reagents were of analytical grade and provided by Tianjin FengChuan chemical reagent Technology Co., Ltd., China. All chemicals and solvents were used without further purification. Compounds 4, 3, RQ and the sensing material poly(MMA-co-RQ) were synthesized by following Scheme 1 and modified reported methods [47,56,57].

Compound 4 was synthesized from rhodamine B via a procedure we have reported [57]. Rhodamine B (2.0078g, 4.52mmol) and 80% hydrazine hydrate (8mL) was dissolved in 40mL methanol. The mixture was refluxed under stirring for 6h in argon atmosphere. After distillation in vacuum, the residue was recrystallized with methanol to give the final product 4 in a yield of 76.9%.

A mixture of 8-hydroxy-2-methylquinoline (4.8163g, 30mmol) and triethylamine (3mL) were dissolved in 40mL toluene and the mixture was stirred at 0°C. After stirring for 30min, 8mL acryloyl chloride was slowly added dropwise and the reaction mixture was stirred for 12h at 20°C. The mixture was filtered and evaporated to generate the crude residue. The final product 3 was obtained by column chromatography (mesh chromatography silica, 1:4 ethyl acetate/light petroleum) in a yield of 47.3%. Characterization of compound 3: 1H NMR: δ H (400MHz, DMSO-d 6, Me4Si): 3.36 (s, 3H), 6.20 (t, 1H), 6.59 (d, 2H), 7.47 (d, 1H), 7.55 (m, 2H), 7.86 (d, 1H), and 8.31 (d, 1H). 13C NMR: δ C (100MHz, DMSO-d 6): 164.71, 159.47, 146.76, 140.37, 136.69, 133.92, 128.23, 127.90, 126.32, 125.85, 123.30, 121.95, 25.72.

A mixture of SeO2 (0.6481g, 5.8mmol), compound 3 (0.9275g, 4.4mmol), and 20mL 1,4-dioxane was refluxed under stirring for 4h in argon atmosphere. The mixture was filtered and evaporated to generate the crude product. The final product 3 was obtained by column chromatography (mesh chromatography silica, 1:15 ethyl acetate/light petroleum) in a yield of. Characterization of compound 2: 1H NMR: δ H (400MHz, DMSO-d 6, Me4Si): 6.27 (t, 1H), 6.64 (d, 2H), 7.78 (d, 1H), 7.84 (m, 1H), 8.07 (d, 1H), 8.70 (d, 1H) and 9.98 (s, 1H). 13C NMR: δ C (100MHz, DMSO-d 6): 193.75, 164.68, 152.43, 147.76, 140.62, 138.84, 134.62, 131.27, 129.86, 127.87, 126.86, 123.42, 118.31.

The mixture of compound 4 (0.2430g, 0.53mmol), compound 2 (0.1260g, 0.59mmol), and 7.5mL ethanol was refluxed under stirring for 12h in argon atmosphere. The crude residue was obtained after distillation in vacuum. The final product RQ was obtained by column chromatography (mesh chromatography silica, 1:1 dichloromethane/light petroleum) in a yield of 84.7%. Characterization of probe 1: HRMS (EI) calcd. for C41H40N5O4 [M+H], 666.3080; found, 666.3098. 1H NMR: δ H (400MHz, CDCl3, Me4Si): 1.18 (q, 12H), 3.34 (t, 8H), 6.26 (q, 2H), 6.52 (d, 2H), 6.57 (m, 2H), 7.15 (m, 2H), 7.27 (m, 1H), 7.40 (m, 1H), 7.53 (m, 3H), 8.05 (m, 3H), 8.12 (m, 2H), and 8.69 (s, 1H). 13C NMR: δ C (100MHz, CDCl3): 165.35, 153.13, 152.62, 152.16, 152.10, 149.07, 146.01, 137.52, 135.83, 133.83, 128.59, 128.40, 128.38, 128.06, 127.86, 123.97, 123.63, 118.81, 117.70, 110.02, 108.07, 105.61, 98.04, 66.13, 44.33, 29.06, 22.62, 12.62.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of electrospinning solution and film
ce:section.ce:para:
0.8g poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) was added to 2.4g DMF solution to prepare precursor solution with concentrations of 3%, 4%, and 5%, respectively. The solutions were stirred for 24h at 20°C. The resulting clear homogenous solutions were used for electrospinning the films. The films with different concentrations were obtained with the same method reported by us [42].

1H NMR and 13C NMR spectra were recorded on a Bruker 400 NMR spectrometer. Chemical shifts are reported in parts per million using tetramethylsilane (TMS) as the internal standard. Mass spectra were obtained on high resolution mass spectrometer (IonSpec4.7 Tesla FTMS-MALDI/DHB).

All spectral characterizations were carried out in HPLC-grade solvents at 20°C within a 10mm quartz cell. UV–vis absorption spectra were measured with a TU-1901 double-beam UV–vis spectrophotometer. The morphologies of the final products were examined using a FEI Quanta 200 scanning electron microscope (SEM), with an acceleration voltage of 25kV.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Concentration dependent morphologies of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film
ce:section.ce:para:
A 5wt% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solution was used for electrospinning the films, and a typical SEM image of this nano film is shown in Fig. 1 . It can be seen that the film was composed of nano particles. In order to study the concentration influence on the nano film morphology, 3wt% and 4wt% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solutions were also applied to electrospin films. As shown in Figs. S1 and S2 in Supporting information, the films were also composed of nano particles. Lower concentrations such as 1wt% and 2% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solutions were also employed for electrospinning the films, and nano films were not obtained. Hence, nano film by electrospinning 5% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solution was used as solid state sensor.

A 5wt% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solution was used for electrospinning the films, and a typical SEM image of this nano film is shown in Fig. 1. It can be seen that the film was composed of nano particles. In order to study the concentration influence on the nano film morphology, 3wt% and 4wt% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solutions were also applied to electrospin films. As shown in Figs. S1 and S2 in Supporting information, the films were also composed of nano particles. Lower concentrations such as 1wt% and 2% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solutions were also employed for electrospinning the films, and nano films were not obtained. Hence, nano film by electrospinning 5% poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) DMF solution was used as solid state sensor.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
UV–vis spectral response of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: In order to gain an insight into the signaling properties of the film toward Fe3+, UV–vis titrations were conducted (Fig. 2 ). When Fe3+ (Fe(NO3)3) was added to the film, a significant increase in the absorption peak at 563nm was observed with the film color changing from white to pink, implying that this film can be a naked-eye sensor toward Fe3+. The nano film exhibited high sensitivity to Fe3+ ion in the 10μM–3.0mM response range (Fig. S3 in Supporting information). Too little Fe3+ ion, such as 1μM cannot make the film response with absorption spectroscopy. Too much Fe3+ ion, shown for 4mM, and the total absorption spectra response range is reduced. The static response time of the nano film was <1min ([Fe3+]=0–20μM), and the higher concentration required a longer response time. As demonstrated in Fig. 3 , in the concentration range of 100μM and 2000μM, the absorption peak of the film at 563nm is in good linear relationship with Fe3+ concentration, implying that Fe3+ can be quantitatively detected in a wider concentration range with UV–vis spectroscopy. From the UV–vis titration experiment, the LOD value was calculated as 1.19μM [58,59]. The possible sensing mechanism was shown in Scheme 2 .

In order to gain an insight into the signaling properties of the film toward Fe3+, UV–vis titrations were conducted (Fig. 2). When Fe3+ (Fe(NO3)3) was added to the film, a significant increase in the absorption peak at 563nm was observed with the film color changing from white to pink, implying that this film can be a naked-eye sensor toward Fe3+. The nano film exhibited high sensitivity to Fe3+ ion in the 10μM–3.0mM response range (Fig. S3 in Supporting information). Too little Fe3+ ion, such as 1μM cannot make the film response with absorption spectroscopy. Too much Fe3+ ion, shown for 4mM, and the total absorption spectra response range is reduced. The static response time of the nano film was <1min ([Fe3+]=0–20μM), and the higher concentration required a longer response time. As demonstrated in Fig. 3, in the concentration range of 100μM and 2000μM, the absorption peak of the film at 563nm is in good linear relationship with Fe3+ concentration, implying that Fe3+ can be quantitatively detected in a wider concentration range with UV–vis spectroscopy. From the UV–vis titration experiment, the LOD value was calculated as 1.19μM [58,59]. The possible sensing mechanism was shown in Scheme 2.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Selectivity study of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: The UV–vis titration of the film with various metal ions and anions was conducted to examine the selectivity of the film. As expected, the film only showed no absorption at 563nm. Upon addition of different metal ions, only Fe3+ can induce remarkable absorption at 563nm of the nano film (up of Fig. 4 and red bars of Fig. 5 ), other representative metal ions, such as Al3+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, K+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Cr3+ showed almost negligible effects on the UV–vis behavior of the film. As for the influence of common anions (PPi, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −), no influence was observed on the detection of Fe3+ except PPi (Fig. S4 in Supporting information and up of Fig. 6 ). Down of Fig. 4 indicates the color change of the film in different conditions. Such results implied that the nano film can be a colorimetric sensor toward Fe3+ with high selectivity.

The UV–vis titration of the film with various metal ions and anions was conducted to examine the selectivity of the film. As expected, the film only showed no absorption at 563nm. Upon addition of different metal ions, only Fe3+ can induce remarkable absorption at 563nm of the nano film (up of Fig. 4 and red bars of Fig. 5), other representative metal ions, such as Al3+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, K+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Cr3+ showed almost negligible effects on the UV–vis behavior of the film. As for the influence of common anions (PPi, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −), no influence was observed on the detection of Fe3+ except PPi (Fig. S4 in Supporting information and up of Fig. 6). Down of Fig. 4 indicates the color change of the film in different conditions. Such results implied that the nano film can be a colorimetric sensor toward Fe3+ with high selectivity.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Anti-disturbance study of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: The competition experiments were conducted in the presence of Fe3+ mixed with other metal ions (Al3+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, K+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Cr3+) and anions (PPi, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −), respectively (green bars of Figs. 5 and 6, down of Fig. 6, and Fig. S5 in Supporting information); no significant absorption spectra and color change was found by comparison with that without the other metal ions and anions except PPi. The competition experiments revealed that the nano film showed remarkable capacity of resisting disturbance toward Fe3+ over common metal ions and anions.

The competition experiments were conducted in the presence of Fe3+ mixed with other metal ions (Al3+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, K+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, and Cr3+) and anions (PPi, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −), respectively (green bars of Figs. 5 and 6, down of Fig. 6, and Fig. S5 in Supporting information); no significant absorption spectra and color change was found by comparison with that without the other metal ions and anions except PPi. The competition experiments revealed that the nano film showed remarkable capacity of resisting disturbance toward Fe3+ over common metal ions and anions.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Counterion effect on the Fe3+-selective properties of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film
ce:section.ce:para: Experiments to explore the counterion effect on the Fe3+-selective properties of the film were also performed (Fig. S6 in Supporting information). Chloride and sulfate had similar influence as nitrate when it comes to ferric salts, demonstrating that the anions do not coordinate the metal ions while the film reacts with Fe3+. This indicates that the film as a colorimetric sensor has a much wider application range in sensing Fe3+ ions.

Experiments to explore the counterion effect on the Fe3+-selective properties of the film were also performed (Fig. S6 in Supporting information). Chloride and sulfate had similar influence as nitrate when it comes to ferric salts, demonstrating that the anions do not coordinate the metal ions while the film reacts with Fe3+. This indicates that the film as a colorimetric sensor has a much wider application range in sensing Fe3+ ions.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Colorimetric sensing properties of Fe3+-containing poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward PPi
ce:section.ce:para: As we have mentioned above, addition of Na4P2O7 water solution can result in the absorption and color of the Fe3+-containing nano film signals identical to those of the film only. That is, the Fe3+-containing nano film can detect PPi calorimetrically.

To investigate the changes in absorption spectrum of the Fe3+-containing nano film upon exposure to PPi, UV–vis titrations were conducted. As shown in up of Fig. 7 , upon addition of Na4P2O7 water solution, absorbance at 563nm decreased and absorption signals the same as those of the film only were restored with the color of the film changing from pink to white gradually, implying that Fe3+ is removed from the Fe3+-containing film by PPi, and the Fe3+-containing film can detect PPi. In concentration range of 50 and 3000μM, the absorbance at 563nm is in good linear relationship with PPi concentration (up of Fig. 7), implying that PPi can be quantitatively detected in a wider concentration range. The LOD value from the UV–vis titration experiment can be as low as 1.75μM. The possible sensing mechanism of the Fe3+-containing film toward PPi was illustrated in Scheme 2.

To examine the selectivity and anti-disturbance capacity toward PPi, further experiments were carried out. Upon addition of different anions, only PPi can induce absorbance at 563nm of the Fe3+-containing nano film decreasing and color changing from pink to white (green bars and down of Fig. 6), other representative anions, such as F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 − showed almost no effects on the UV–vis and color behavior of the Fe3+-containing film, implying high selectivity of the Fe3+-containing film toward PPi. The competition experiments were conducted in the presence of Fe3+ and PPi mixed with many anions (F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, H2PO4 −, CH3COO−, HSO4 −, NO3 −, SCN−, SO4 2−, HPO4 2−, S2−, NO2 −, SO3 2−, HSO3 −, ClO4 −, N3 −, SiO3 2−, S2O3 2−, and ClO3 −), respectively (green bars and down of Fig. 8 ). No significant absorption spectra and color change were found by comparison with that without the other anions, which revealed strong anti-disturbance capacity of the Fe3+-containing film toward PPi over other common anions.

ce:section.ce:section-title:
Reusability study of poly(MMA-
co
-RQ) nano film toward Fe3+ and further PPi
ce:section.ce:para: The reusability of material is very important for incorporating them into a sensing device or directly using them as a reusable test strip, which requires that the sensing behavior should be reversible. The reversibility study of sensing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) was carried out for the film. A 4M Na4P2O7 water solution was added to the Fe3+-containing film. Absorption and color signal identical to those of the film only were restored (Fig. 7), demonstrating that Fe3+ is removed from the Fe3+-containing film by PPi, and the binding constant of Fe3+ with PPi is larger than that with the film. That is, the binding of the film and Fe3+ is really chemically reversible. Furthermore, after addition of ferric salt solution to the Fe3+-containing film mixture with PPi, the absorption signal identical to that of the film Fe3+-containing was obtained (Fig. S7 in Supporting information). Such results indicate that both the nano films before and after Fe3+-contaminating are reusable for the detection of Fe3+ and PPi, respectively.

The reusability of material is very important for incorporating them into a sensing device or directly using them as a reusable test strip, which requires that the sensing behavior should be reversible. The reversibility study of sensing Fe3+ (5×10−3 M) was carried out for the film. A 4M Na4P2O7 water solution was added to the Fe3+-containing film. Absorption and color signal identical to those of the film only were restored (Fig. 7), demonstrating that Fe3+ is removed from the Fe3+-containing film by PPi, and the binding constant of Fe3+ with PPi is larger than that with the film. That is, the binding of the film and Fe3+ is really chemically reversible. Furthermore, after addition of ferric salt solution to the Fe3+-containing film mixture with PPi, the absorption signal identical to that of the film Fe3+-containing was obtained (Fig. S7 in Supporting information). Such results indicate that both the nano films before and after Fe3+-contaminating are reusable for the detection of Fe3+ and PPi, respectively.

In summary, based on rhodamine and quinoline, a novel colorimetric nano film chemosensor toward Fe3+ and further for PPi has been developed by copolymerization and electrospinning. As a solid-state sensor, the nano film exhibits highly selective recognition of Fe3+ ions and further of PPi over various environmentally and biologically relevant metal ions and anions. The sensor can detect Fe3+ and further PPi quantitatively in concentrations range of 100–2000μM and 50–3000μM, respectively, and the LOD could be 1.19μM for Fe3+ and 1.75μM for PPi. In addition, both the nano films before and after Fe3+-contaminating are reusable for the detection of Fe3+ and PPi, respectively.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors thank National Natural Science Foundation of China (U1504203 and J1210060), Key Laboratory of Photochemical Conversion and Optoelectronic Materials and Zhengzhou University for financial support.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found, in the online version, at http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.snb.2015.11.105.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: The following are the supplementary data to this article:

A dual-responsive chromo-fluorescent probe for detection of Zn2+ and Fe3+ via two different approaches

Tuning colourimetric indicator displacement assays for naked-eye sensing of pyrophosphate in aqueous media

Hydrophilic non-wovens made of cross-linked fully-hydrolyzed poly(vinyl alcohol) electrospun nanofibers

Highly sensitive oxygen sensors based on Cu(I) complex-polystyrene composite nanofibrous membranes prepared by electrospinning
Novel signal-amplifying fluorescent nanofibers for naked-eye-based ultrasensitive detection of buried explosives and explosive vapors
A facile and sensitive fluorescent sensor using electrospun nanofibrous film for nitroaromatic explosive detection
Ultra-fast opto-chemical sensors by using electrospun nanofibers as sensing layers
Electrospun 1,4-DHAQ-doped cellulose nanofiber films for reusable fluorescence detection of trace Cu2+ and further for Cr3+
Colorimetric and fluorescent nanofibrous film as a chemosensor for Hg2+ in aqueous solution prepared by electrospinning and host-guest interaction
Rhodamine derivative-modified filter papers for colorimetric and fluorescent detection of Hg2+ in aqueous media
Highly sensitive and selective Cu2+ sensor based on electrospun rhodamine dye doped poly(ether sulfones) nanofibers
Naked-eye detection of C1–C4 alcohols based on ground-state intramolecular proton transfer

Near-infrared fluorescent probe for detection of thiophenols in water samples and living cells

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Zhanxian Li received her Ph.D. degree in 2006 from Chinese Academy of Sciences. She is an associate professor of College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her current research interests are mainly in fluorescent chemosensors and molecular recognition.

Haixia Li received her B.S. degree in 2015. She is currently a M.S. candidate at College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her research interests focus on developing fluorescent chemosensors.

Caixia Shi received her B.S. degree in 2012. She is currently a M.S. candidate at College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her research interests focus on developing fluorescent chemosensors.

Wenying Zhang received her M.S. degree in 2013. She is currently a M.S. candidate at College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her research interests focus on developing fluorescent chemosensors.

Wan Zhou received her B.S. degree in 2012. She is currently a M.S. candidate at College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her research interests focus on developing fluorescent chemosensors.
Liuhe Wei is a professor of College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. Her current research interests focus on functional polymers and supermolecular chemistry.

Mingming Yu is an associate professor of College of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, Zhengzhou University. His current research interests are mainly in fluorescent chemosensors and functional polymers.
